error.name_required: Please enter a name for the controller.
error.diff_off_greater_than_diff_low: Stop difference must be ≤ Low-power difference. Otherwise the OFF zone would overlap with the LOW zone.
error.diff_on_less_than_diff_off: Turn-on difference must be ≥ Turn-off difference. Otherwise the hysteresis band would be inverted.

user.title: My AC Controller — Basic Setup
user.description:
## Welcome to My AC Controller

This integration solves a common problem: **air conditioner built-in sensors are often inaccurate** due to installation position (near ceiling, behind curtains, etc.).

### How it works
1. You provide an **external temperature sensor** placed at a representative location in the room — this becomes the **trusted temperature (T_trust)**.
2. The integration reads the **AC's internal sensor (T_ac)** for reference.
3. It compares T_trust against your **desired temperature (T_expectation)** and controls the AC accordingly.

### Settings on this page

- **Name** — A friendly name, e.g. "Living Room AC Controller"
- **AC Climate Entity** — The existing climate entity that controls your air conditioner
- **External Temperature Sensor** — A temperature sensor entity placed where you want accurate readings (Zigbee/ZWave/WiFi sensor, or an input_number)
- **AC Type** — see below

### AC Type: which one should I choose?

| Type | Best for |
|------|----------|
| **Inverter** | Modern ACs that vary compressor speed. 3-zone control with low-power mode for precise temperature maintenance. |
| **Fixed-speed** | Older ACs that are either fully ON or OFF. Hysteresis control to prevent short-cycling. |

> 💡 If your AC remote has 0.5°C temperature steps and runs quietly sometimes, it is likely an inverter AC.

bp_settings.title: Inverter Mode — Threshold Settings
bp_settings.description:
## Inverter AC: 3-Zone Control

Your AC will operate in one of three zones depending on how far the room temperature is from your target:

|Diff|: `0 ── Diff_off ── Diff_low ── ∞`
Zone:  `FAN     LOW        HIGH`

### Zone behavior

| Zone | Condition | What happens |
|------|-----------|--------------|
| **FAN** | Room temp within `Diff_off` of target | Compressor stops, AC runs fan-only to circulate air without cooling/heating. Prevents overshooting while keeping air fresh. |
| **LOW** | Room temp between `Diff_off` and `Diff_low` | AC runs at minimum inverter frequency. Target is set close to current room temp for gentle fine-tuning. |
| **HIGH** | Room temp more than `Diff_low` away | AC runs at full capacity. Target = your desired temperature. |

### Example (Cooling, target 25°C, Diff_off=0.3, Diff_low=1.0)

| Room Temp | \|Diff\| | Zone | Behavior |
|-----------|--------|------|----------|
| 28.0°C | 3.0 | HIGH | Full power → target 25°C |
| 25.8°C | 0.8 | LOW | Gentle cooling, target ≈ current temp |
| 25.2°C | 0.2 | FAN | Fan only, compressor off |

---

### Settings

- **Stop Difference (Diff_off)** — When temperature is this close to target, switch to fan-only mode (compressor stops). *Must be ≤ Diff_low.*
- **Low-Power Difference (Diff_low)** — When temperature is closer than this (but > Diff_off), use low power.
- **Temperature Step** — AC target rounding step in low-power mode. Match your AC's supported increments (usually 0.5 or 1.0°C).
- **Round Direction** — In low power: **Up** = gentler, **Down** = slightly more aggressive.

> 💡 **Tuning tips:** If temperature oscillates, increase `Diff_low`. If AC switches to fan too soon, decrease `Diff_off`.

dp_settings.title: Fixed-Speed Mode — Threshold Settings
dp_settings.description:
## Fixed-Speed AC: Hysteresis Control

Fixed-speed ACs are either fully ON or fully OFF. Hysteresis prevents rapid cycling that can damage the compressor:

- When |Diff| reaches `Diff_on` → AC turns **ON**
- When |Diff| drops to `Diff_off` → AC turns **OFF**
- Between `Diff_off` and `Diff_on` → stays in current state (hysteresis band)

### Example (Cooling, target 25°C, Diff_on=1.0, Diff_off=0.3)

| Time | Room | \|Diff\| | State | Action |
|------|------|--------|-------|--------|
| Morning | 28.0°C | 3.0 | OFF | → **ON** (\|Diff\| ≥ 1.0) |
| Midday | 25.5°C | 0.5 | ON | hold (in band) |
| Afternoon | 25.2°C | 0.2 | ON | → **OFF** (\|Diff\| ≤ 0.3) |
| Evening | 25.8°C | 0.8 | OFF | hold (in band) |
| Night | 26.3°C | 1.3 | OFF | → **ON** again |

---

### Settings

- **Turn-On Difference (Diff_on)** — When temperature deviates this far, turn AC on. *Must be ≥ Diff_off.*
- **Turn-Off Difference (Diff_off)** — When temperature is this close to target, turn AC off.

> 💡 **Tuning tips:** Keep `Diff_on` > `Diff_off` by at least 0.5°C for a healthy hysteresis band. Larger `Diff_on` = longer cycles, less frequent starts.

common.title: Common Settings
common.description:
## General Settings

### Poll Interval

How often (in seconds) the controller checks temperatures and adjusts the AC.

- **10–15s** — Most responsive. For rooms with fast temperature changes.
- **30s** *(default)* — Balanced. Works well for most rooms.
- **60–120s** — Larger rooms with slow temperature changes.
- **180–300s** — Minimal overhead. Very stable environments only.

> 💡 The controller also reacts immediately when you change the target temperature or HVAC mode — the poll interval is only the background check frequency.

error.diff_off_greater_than_diff_low: Stop difference must be ≤ Low-power difference.
error.diff_on_less_than_diff_off: Turn-on difference must be ≥ Turn-off difference.

init.title: My AC Controller — Options
init.description:
Which settings would you like to adjust?

- **Mode Thresholds** — Temperature difference thresholds that control AC behavior.
- **Common Settings** — Poll interval.

mode_settings.title: Mode Thresholds
mode_settings.description: Adjust the temperature thresholds that control your AC. See the setup wizard descriptions for detailed guidance on each field.

common_settings.title: Common Settings
common_settings.description:
### Poll Interval

How often (seconds) the controller checks temperatures.

- **10–30s** — Responsive, for fast-changing rooms.
- **30–60s** — Balanced (default: 30s).
- **60–300s** — For stable environments.
